description_long: This object is a vintage cathedral-style radio, characterized by its arched wooden cabinet and ornate front grille. The front features a decorative metallic grille with a fan-like pattern, covering a fabric speaker mesh, and two round control knobs below. The wood grain is rich and dark, with a glossy finish that highlights the natural patterns of the veneer. The sides are smoothly curved and display a striped wood pattern, while the back is plain with a similar wood texture. The base is flat and sturdy, designed to rest securely on a surface. This radio evokes early 20th-century design, blending functionality with decorative craftsmanship.
description: A vintage cathedral-style radio with a dark, glossy wooden cabinet, ornate metallic grille, and two control knobs on the front.
description_view_0: The front view shows the arched shape, metallic grille, speaker mesh, and two control knobs.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the smooth, curved profile and striped wood grain pattern.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a plain, dark wood surface with no visible controls or openings.
description_view_3: The opposite side view mirrors the other side, showing the same curved, striped wood finish.
